response.justification: The candidate is a strong, versatile engineer with genuine systems-level experience, but the role's core requirement is deep, current C++ expertise for industrial automation. His only professional C++ work was a roughly seven-month stint at National Instruments in 2014-2015, and C++ does not appear in his current skills list; his last decade has centered on web and full-stack work in TypeScript, Node.js, and DLang. He also shows no exposure to MQTT, industrial protocols, IEC 62443, or Secure Development Lifecycle practices, all of which are central to this position.
strengths: Real high-performance systems experience: low-latency services handling millions of requests per second using async I/O, fibers, and caching
Strong engineering craftsmanship culture: TDD, CI/CD, trunk-based development, and open-source authorship of testing tools
Proven mentor and educator through workshops, courses, and conference talks, matching the role's mentoring responsibility
concerns: Minimal professional C++ experience: one short engagement a decade ago, with no evidence of modern C++/STL depth required for the role
No industrial automation, IoT, MQTT, IEC 62443, or secure development lifecycle background
Recent career trajectory is web/full-stack (TypeScript, Node.js, React), diverging from networked multi-threaded C++ systems work
